goal: Architect a ROS2 deploy bridge for the G1: on-robot Jetson runs rclpy + ONNX Runtime (no Isaac Sim); desktop Isaac Sim uses ros2_bridge only for viz.
thoughts: Isaac Sim does NOT run on aarch64/Orin for deploy — reject 'parity' request. Split: desktop Isaac Sim + omni.isaac.ros2_bridge for viz only; on-robot = plain rclpy + onnxruntime. JointState.name order is NOT guaranteed by ROS2 convention — MUST reorder by name to match training-time joint order or the policy sees permuted obs. Action clip + rate limit on the robot side: a safe-in-sim action can still be violent on hardware. Watchdog: timeout on /joint_states -> publish zero velocity.
code:
# 1. Scope of Isaac Sim ros2_bridge (viz desktop) vs on-robot stack
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim ROS2 bridge architecture Jetson Orin deploy ONNX Runtime rclpy")

# 2. Check for any shipped G1 ROS2 deploy example
catalog_search(query="Unitree G1 ROS2 Humble deploy example onnx inference")

# 3. Desktop Isaac Sim: enable ros2_bridge for viz only
setup_ros2_bridge(
    stage_path="<DEMO_STAGE>",
    publish_joint_states=True,
    publish_tf=True,
    namespace="isaac_viz"
)

# 4. Orin runtime spec
lookup_product_spec(query="Jetson Orin ROS2 Humble onnxruntime-gpu TensorRT 10 aarch64")

failure_modes: Running Isaac Sim on Orin for 'parity' — unsupported aarch64, wastes a sprint
Trusting JointState.name order — silent obs permutation, policy acts weirdly
Missing the watchdog — ROS2 drop means last action latches, robot runs away
Skipping action clip/rate-limit — sim-safe action becomes hardware-violent
